summary: Gruber introduces Michael Tsai, a Mac software developer behind utilities like DropDMG and BBAutoComplete, and editor of the About This Particular Macintosh e-zine. The post serves as a preface to an email interview with Tsai, primarily focused on promoting his latest release, SpamSieve 2, a Bayesian spam filtering utility. Gruber shares his personal experience using SpamSieve 2 for over a month, reporting it caught all but 10 of 1,981 spams. He gives a strong endorsement of the software's ease of use, speed, and broad email client compatibility.
key_points: Michael Tsai is a prolific Mac developer and publisher of the ATPM e-zine
SpamSieve 2 uses Bayesian statistical filtering among other strategies to combat spam
Gruber's personal testing showed SpamSieve catching 99.5% of spam over a month
SpamSieve works with every major Mac OS X email client except Apple Mail
money_quotes: Its efficacy has bordered on the incredible: flagging all but 10 of 1981 spams.
SpamSieve is easy to set up, runs quickly, and works with every major Mac OS X email client except Apple Mail.
Needless to say, I highly recommend SpamSieve.
key_insight: SpamSieve 2 represents the kind of thoughtful, effective Mac utility software that solves a real problem with near-perfect accuracy.
